thesis: Q1 2026 beat: 32% YoY revenue growth, >$1B quarterly revenue for first time. FedRAMP High certification opens government/federal market. AI-driven observability platform with strong customer expansion (4,550 new $100k+ ARR customers, 21% growth). AI agentic deployment acceleration expected to drive usage-based growth. Partnership with Sakana AI. Long-term thesis: mission-critical monitoring for cloud-native enterprises with expanding AI infrastructure.

First-in-disease treatment for MASLD/NASH (liver disease) with Rezdiffra achieving blockbuster status ($1.1B trailing 12-month sales, 127% YoY growth in Q1 2026). 13 analysts average PT $671.58 (+33% upside from ~$505). April 2026 was strongest month for new prescriptions since launch. Profitability inevitable post-2026. F4C cirrhosis outcomes trial readout in 2027 is a potential market-doubling catalyst by expanding label to well-compensated cirrhosis.

Q1 2026 EPS $1.06 beat vs $0.60-0.64 consensus. Iran war driving Brent oil to $102-107/barrel — structural tailwind for US upstream producers. Berkshire Hathaway backing (top 10 holding retained by Greg Abel). Debt reduction progressing ($7.1B repaid toward $10B target). Raymond James PT $75 Outperform (+26% upside from $59.65). YTD up 37%. CEO Vicki Hollub retiring June 1; COO Richard Jackson taking over — orderly transition.

Q2 2026 EPS $3.33 (+38% YoY), revenue $11.2B (+17% YoY), both beating estimates. Payment network duopoly (Visa + Mastercard) with durable competitive advantage — not exposed to credit risk unlike banks. Analyst average PT $390.67 (+17% upside from $332.50); strong buy consensus (21 buy, 2 hold, 0 sell). Truist raised PT to $371 on May 12. Benefits from global consumer spending and digital payments growth. Hold for 6-12 months.

Defensive consumer staples leader benefiting from tariff-driven trade-down as consumers seek value. Consistent market share gains across grocery and general merchandise. Q1 FY2027 earnings due May 21 — watch closely. Portfolio diversification anchor during uncertain macro environment (Moody's downgrade, rate hike concerns). Strong supply chain and private label expansion. Defensive buffer for portfolio in risk-off periods.
